factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed with increases in average yield offsetting volume declines in certain lines of business.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2016 increased 1.2% due primarily to increases in average yield in all lines of business. These increases were partially offset by declines in our energy services business, as well as volume declines primarily in our residential collection line of business and special waste volume declines in our landfill line of business.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Demand trends are mixed with both increases in average yield and volume declines in certain segments.

factors.summary: Pricing power is partially effective as increases in average yield have been offset by volume declines in certain segments.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2016 increased 1.2% due primarily to increases in average yield in all lines of business. These increases were partially offset by declines in our energy services business, as well as volume declines primarily in our residential collection line of business and special waste volume declines in our landfill line of business.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is partially effective as increases in average yield have been offset by volume declines in certain segments.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments as average yield increases and volume increases in certain lines offset declines in others.

evidence.text: Revenue for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 increased 1.4% due primarily to increases in average yield in all lines of business and volume increases in our large-container industrial collection and disposal lines of business. Volume increases in our landfill line of business were primarily attributable to increased construction and demolition and municipal solid waste volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments with average yield increases and volume increases in certain lines.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is improving as increases in average yield and volume in certain lines offset declines in others, excluding acquisitions.

evidence.text: Revenue for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 increased 1.4% due primarily to increases in average yield in all lines of business and volume increases in our large-container industrial collection and disposal lines of business.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is improving with increases in average yield and volume in certain lines.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are mixed with increases in certain lines offset by declines in others.

evidence.text: Volume increases in our landfill line of business were primarily attributable to increased construction and demolition and municipal solid waste volumes. Additionally, volume increased due to an additional workday for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 compared to the same period in 2015. These increases were partially offset by declines in our energy services business, as well as volume declines primarily in our residential collection line of business and special waste volume declines in our landfill line of business.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are mixed with increases in certain lines offset by declines in others.

factors.summary: Input costs are decreasing, particularly in fuel costs and risk management expenses.

evidence.text: Cost of operations favorably impacted operating income margin for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016 , primarily due to lower fuel costs resulting from lower prices of diesel fuel and lower risk management expenses. These favorable items were partially offset by higher cost of goods sold and landfill operating costs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are decreasing, particularly in fuel costs and risk management expenses.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to cost reductions and favorable developments.

evidence.text: Operating income in Group 2 increased from $239.0 million for the three months ended September 30, 2015 , or an 18.9% operating margin, to $261.8 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 , or a 20.4% operating margin. Operating income in Group 2 increased from $717.4 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2015 , or a 19.4% operating margin, to $748.4 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , or a 20.0% operating margin.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to cost reductions and favorable developments.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in fuel costs and risk management expenses.

evidence.text: Cost of operations favorably impacted operating income margin for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016 , primarily due to lower fuel costs resulting from lower prices of diesel fuel and lower risk management expenses. These favorable items were partially offset by higher cost of goods sold and landfill operating costs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, particularly in fuel costs and risk management expenses.

factors.summary: Management appears confident with increased share repurchases and dividend payments.

evidence.text: During the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , we repurchased 6.5 million shares of our stock for $306.6 million. Dividends paid were $309.9 million and $295.0 million during the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows Used in Financing Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's increased share repurchases and dividend payments indicate confidence in the company's future.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable claims experience and increased risk management expenses.

evidence.text: During the three months ended September 30, 2016 , we recognized increased risk management expenses primarily due to favorable actuarial developments in our workers' compensation program recorded during the three months ended September 30, 2015 that were less favorable for the same period in 2016 as well as unfavorable claims experience in our employee-related health care benefits programs in 2016 as compared to 2015.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable claims experience and increased risk management expenses pose significant near-term risks.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel costs and risk management expenses, which have a significant impact on near-term results.

evidence.text: Cost of operations favorably impacted operating income margin for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2016 , primarily due to lower fuel costs resulting from lower prices of diesel fuel and lower risk management expenses. These favorable items were partially offset by higher cost of goods sold and landfill operating costs.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Fuel costs and risk management expenses are key sensitivities, but their impact is mixed based on current conditions.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures during the nine months ended September 30, 2016 were $738.7 million , compared with $732.0 million in the same period in 2015 .
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows Used in Investing Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditures signal confidence in future growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is improving with a lower interest cost and better EBITDA to interest ratio.

evidence.text: As of September 30, 2016 , our EBITDA to interest ratio was 6.90 compared to the 3.00 minimum required by the covenants, and our total debt to EBITDA ratio was 3.03 compared to the 3.50 maximum allowed by the covenants.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved debt leverage with a strong EBITDA to interest ratio and lower debt to EBITDA indicates financial stability.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing with higher share repurchases and dividends, indicating management confidence.

evidence.text: During the nine months ended September 30, 2016 , we repurchased 6.5 million shares of our stock for $306.6 million. Dividends paid were $309.9 million and $295.0 million during the nine months ended September 30, 2016 and 2015 , respectively.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows Used in Financing Activities

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher share repurchases and dividends reflect management's confidence in the company's prospects.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving with positive cash flows from operations.

evidence.text: Cash provided by operating activities was $1,359.6 million for the nine months ended September 30, 2016.
evidence.subsection: Cash Flows Provided by Operating Activities

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Positive cash flows from operations demonstrate strong free cash flow generation, supporting growth and stability.

factors.summary: Environmental compliance costs are significant, particularly related to the Bridgeton Landfill.

evidence.text: Operating loss in our Corporate Entities increased from $16.9 million for the three months ended September 30, 2015 to $79.0 million for the three months ended September 30, 2016 , primarily due to an insurance recovery of $50.0 million related to our Bridgeton Landfill that was recorded during the three months ended September 30, 2015 .

factors.summary: Litigation exposure exists with ongoing remediation efforts and legal settlements.

evidence.text: It is reasonably possible that we will need to adjust our accrued landfill and environmental liabilities to reflect the effects of new or additional information, to the extent that such information impacts the costs, timing or duration of the required actions. Future changes in our estimates of the costs, timing or duration of the required actions could have a material adverse effect on our consolidated financial position, results of operations or cash flows.
evidence.subsection: Remediation and Other Charges for Landfill Matters

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: The potential for material adverse effects on financial position and operations indicates a moderate headwind.
